subject: LIGO/Virgo S190524q: Retraction of GW compact binary merger candidate
editedBy: Judith Racusin at NASA/GSFC <judith.racusin@nasa.gov> on behalf of Tyler Barna at University of Minnesota <tylerpbarna@gmail.com>
submitter: Brandon Piotrzkowski at U of Wisconsin-Milwaukee  <piotrzk3@uwm.edu>
body:
The LIGO Scientific Collaboration and the Virgo Collaboration report:


The trigger S190524q is no longer considered to be a gravitational

wave signal.


The data in LIGO-Livingston were highly non-stationary leading up to

the reported trigger time.
